name: Returning user scans the KPI strip and active jobs
description: A loaded dashboard for a contractor mid-season. KPIs, active jobs, outstanding balances, and quotes awaiting all populated. The page should answer 'what should I look at first today?' in under five seconds.

persona.name: Diego Martínez
persona.context: Two-truck crew, eight active jobs, three quotes out, two invoices overdue.

preconditions: Account has 8 active jobs, 3 outstanding invoices totalling $14,200, 4 quotes awaiting
Recent activity in the last 24h: 2 quote opens, 1 deposit payment

steps.expect: KPIs above the fold; numbers match the underlying API responses
Hover state is visible (not invisible on light backgrounds); cursor changes to pointer if clickable
Navigates to the relevant invoice or client page within ~200ms
Scroll position and section ordering are preserved; no full re-fetch flash

evaluationCriteria.check: Currency formatting uses thousands separators and no trailing cents (e.g. '$14,200')
The eye is drawn first to the most actionable item (overdue invoice or stale quote), not the prettiest tile
Due-date relative labels ('Today', 'Tomorrow', 'Yesterday', '3 days ago') match the user's local timezone
KPI tiles are real headings, not divs styled to look like headings
